termination_reason: 累计 202 ≥ 40 条 → 立即停

items.title: The human disease network
items.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
items.summary: Constructs the human disease network connecting diseases based on shared genetic origins, revealing that diseases are highly interconnected and that disease genes cluster in the interactome.

items.title: Limits of Predictability in Human Mobility
items.summary: Quantifies the fundamental limits of predictability in human mobility, showing that despite the randomness of individual movements, 93% of human mobility is predictable.

items.title: The Product Space Conditions the Development of Nations
items.summary: Analyses the product space connecting goods traded globally, showing that a country's development trajectory depends on its position in this network.

items.title: The origin of bursts and heavy tails in human dynamics
items.summary: Shows that the heavy tails and bursty inter-event times observed in human communication patterns arise from queuing and priority-based decision processes.

items.title: Mean-field theory for scale-free random networks
items.journal: Physica A Statistical Mechanics and its Applications
items.summary: Derives the mean-field theory for scale-free random networks, providing analytical solutions for the degree distribution and showing that preferential attachment yields power-law scaling.

items.title: Hierarchical organization in complex networks
items.journal: Physical review. E, Statistical physics, plasmas, fluids, and related interdisciplinary topics
items.summary: Demonstrates that many complex networks exhibit hierarchical organization, combining scale-free topology with high clustering coefficients through a deterministic model.

items.title: Scale-Free Networks: A Decade and Beyond
items.summary: Reviews the first decade of scale-free network research, covering the ubiquity of power-law degree distributions and the impact of the Barabási-Albert model across disciplines.

items.title: Structure and tie strengths in mobile communication networks
items.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
items.summary: Analyzes mobile communication networks to reveal the relationship between network structure and tie strengths, showing how weak ties bridge communities.

items.title: Interactome Networks and Human Disease
items.summary: Reviews how protein interaction networks (interactomes) provide a framework for understanding human disease, identifying disease modules and network-based drug targets.

items.title: Drug—target network
items.summary: Maps the network of FDA-approved drugs and their known protein targets, revealing the modular structure of drug-target interactions and polypharmacology.

items.title: Quantifying social group evolution
items.summary: Quantifies the evolution of social groups over time using mobile phone data, revealing the dynamic processes governing group formation and dissolution.

items.title: Uncovering disease-disease relationships through the incomplete interactome
items.summary: Develops methods to uncover disease-disease relationships through analysis of the incomplete human interactome, revealing molecular connections between seemingly unrelated conditions.

items.title: A universal model for mobility and migration patterns
items.summary: Develops a universal model for human mobility and migration patterns that captures the scaling properties of movement across different scales.

items.title: A Proteome-Scale Map of the Human Interactome Network
items.summary: Presents a proteome-scale map of the human interactome network, systematically mapping protein-protein interactions to understand cellular organization and disease.

items.title: High-Quality Binary Protein Interaction Map of the Yeast Interactome Network
items.summary: Provides a high-quality binary protein interaction map of the yeast interactome network, establishing the gold standard for interactome mapping.

items.title: Science of science
items.summary: Provides a comprehensive review of the science of science field, covering the quantitative understanding of scientific careers, impact, collaboration, and the dynamics of knowledge creation.

items.title: Topology of Evolving Networks: Local Events and Universality
items.summary: Studies the topology of evolving networks, showing that local events and preferential attachment lead to universal scaling properties in network structure.

items.title: Modelling the scaling properties of human mobility
items.summary: Models the scaling properties of human mobility, reproducing the characteristic displacement and waiting time distributions observed in real trajectory data.

items.title: Universal resilience patterns in complex networks
items.summary: Derives universal resilience patterns in complex networks, showing that network topology determines the resilience of ecological, biological, and social systems to perturbations.

items.title: Competition and multiscaling in evolving networks
items.summary: Analyzes competition in evolving networks and demonstrates multiscaling behavior where nodes with different ages and fitness levels exhibit distinct scaling exponents.

items.title: Historical comparison of gender inequality in scientific careers across countries and disciplines
items.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
items.summary: Provides a large-scale historical comparison of gender inequality in scientific careers across countries and disciplines, documenting persistent gaps and regional variation.

items.title: Evidence for Network Evolution in an Arabidopsis Interactome Map
items.summary: Presents evidence for the evolutionary rewiring of protein interaction networks across species using the Arabidopsis interactome map.

items.title: An empirical framework for binary interactome mapping
items.summary: Develops an empirical framework for systematic binary interactome mapping, establishing quality standards for protein-protein interaction data.

items.title: Quantifying Long-Term Scientific Impact
items.summary: Quantifies long-term scientific impact using bibliometric measures, showing that early-career publishing patterns predict long-term success.

items.title: Network-based prediction of drug combinations
items.summary: Develops a network-based method to predict drug combinations with synergistic effects, leveraging the human interactome to identify effective multi-target therapies.

items.title: Bose-Einstein Condensation in Complex Networks
items.summary: Introduces a model where network evolution mirrors Bose-Einstein condensation, with winner-take-all effects where a single node captures a finite fraction of all connections.

items.title: A Protein–Protein Interaction Network for Human Inherited Ataxias and Disorders of Purkinje Cell Degeneration
items.summary: Maps the protein-protein interaction network for inherited ataxias and Purkinje cell degeneration disorders, revealing disease mechanisms.

items.title: Canonical genetic signatures of the adult human brain
items.summary: Identifies canonical genetic signatures across the adult human brain, revealing transcriptional patterns linked to brain structure and function.

items.title: Experimental Determination and System Level Analysis of Essential Genes in Escherichia coli MG1655
items.summary: Determines the set of essential genes in E. coli through systematic gene knockout experiments, linking essentiality to network topology.

items.title: Global organization of metabolic fluxes in the bacterium Escherichia coli
items.summary: Reveals the global organization of metabolic fluxes in E. coli, showing that metabolic activity follows a hierarchical, modular structure.

items.title: A Dynamic Network Approach for the Study of Human Phenotypes
items.summary: Develops a dynamic network approach to study human phenotypes, connecting temporal phenotypic data to underlying molecular networks.

items.title: Modeling bursts and heavy tails in human dynamics
items.summary: Develops models explaining bursts and heavy tails in human communication dynamics, linking individual decision queuing to macroscopic temporal patterns.

items.title: Human mobility, social ties, and link prediction
items.summary: Connects human mobility patterns with social network ties, demonstrating that short-range mobility is primarily driven by social relationships.

items.title: Modeling the Internet's large-scale topology
items.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
items.summary: Models the large-scale topology of the Internet, showing that it follows a scale-free structure with highly connected hubs and hierarchical organization.

items.title: Small but slow world: How network topology and burstiness slow down spreading
items.summary: Shows how network topology combined with bursty interaction patterns slow down spreading processes, challenging conventional assumptions about small-world efficiency.

items.title: Control principles of complex systems
items.summary: Reviews control principles of complex systems, covering structural controllability, target control, and the observability of complex networks.

items.title: Human symptoms–disease network
items.summary: Constructs a bipartite network linking human symptoms to diseases, enabling network-based analysis of symptom co-occurrence and molecular disease relationships.

items.title: Widespread Macromolecular Interaction Perturbations in Human Genetic Disorders
items.summary: Reveals how human genetic disorders cause widespread perturbations in macromolecular interaction networks, beyond individual protein effects.

items.title: Quantifying the evolution of individual scientific impact
items.summary: Quantifies how individual scientific impact evolves over a career using publication and citation data, revealing universal career dynamics.

items.title: Functional and topological characterization of protein interaction networks
items.summary: Characterizes the functional and topological properties of protein interaction networks, linking network position to biological function.

items.title: Network-based in silico drug efficacy screening
items.summary: Uses network-based in silico methods to predict drug efficacy by measuring the network proximity between drug targets and disease modules.

items.title: Human disease classification in the postgenomic era: A complex systems approach to human pathobiology
items.summary: Proposes a network-based approach to human disease classification in the postgenomic era using complex systems concepts.

items.title: Halting viruses in scale-free networks
items.journal: Physical review. E, Statistical physics, plasmas, fluids, and related interdisciplinary topics
items.summary: Demonstrates that targeted immunization of highly connected hubs can halt virus spread in scale-free networks, restoring a finite epidemic threshold.

items.title: Measuring preferential attachment in evolving networks
items.summary: Empirically measures preferential attachment in evolving real-world networks, confirming the mechanism that generates scale-free degree distributions.

items.title: Network-based approach to prediction and population-based validation of in silico drug repurposing
items.summary: Develops and validates a network-based approach to predict drug repurposing opportunities using population-level healthcare data.

items.title: Network science
items.journal: Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society A Mathematical Physical and Engineering Sciences
items.summary: Provides a comprehensive introduction to network science, covering the fundamental concepts of network structure, dynamics, and applications across disciplines.

items.title: Burstiness and memory in complex systems
items.summary: Characterizes burstiness and memory effects in complex systems, showing that temporal correlations between events significantly affect dynamics.

items.title: Network Medicine — From Obesity to the “Diseasome”
items.summary: Explores connections between obesity and the diseasome using network medicine, revealing shared molecular pathways between metabolic and other diseases.

items.title: Network Medicine Framework for Identifying Drug Repurposing Opportunities for COVID-19
items.summary: Applies network medicine principles to identify drug repurposing opportunities for COVID-19 by analyzing host-virus protein interactions.

items.title: Observability of complex systems
items.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
items.summary: Introduces the observability problem for complex networks, deriving conditions under which the internal states of a network can be inferred from external measurements.

items.title: The network takeover
items.summary: Argues that network thinking has fundamentally transformed our understanding of complex systems across physics, biology, and social sciences.

items.title: Dynamic Scaling of Ion-Sputtered Surfaces
items.summary: Discovers dynamic scaling behavior in ion-sputtered surfaces, revealing universal growth and roughening kinetics.

items.title: Life's Complexity Pyramid
items.summary: Proposes a complexity pyramid framework for understanding the hierarchical organization of life from molecules to ecosystems.

items.title: The implications of human metabolic network topology for disease comorbidity
items.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
items.summary: Shows that the topology of the human metabolic network predicts disease comorbidity patterns through shared metabolic pathways.

items.title: The fundamental advantages of temporal networks
items.summary: Reveals the fundamental advantages of temporal networks over static networks for information processing and dynamical processes.

items.title: Understanding the Spreading Patterns of Mobile Phone Viruses
items.summary: Models the spreading patterns of mobile phone viruses using network theory, predicting epidemic thresholds on real communication networks.

items.title: Spectra of “real-world” graphs: Beyond the semicircle law
items.journal: Physical review. E, Statistical physics, plasmas, fluids, and related interdisciplinary topics
items.summary: Studies the structural and functional properties of networks in the context of Physical review. E, Statistical physics, plasmas, fluids, and related interdisciplinary topics, contributing to the understanding of complex systems.

items.title: Returners and explorers dichotomy in human mobility
items.summary: Introduces the dichotomy between 'returners' (individuals who return to frequently visited locations) and 'explorers' in human mobility patterns.

items.title: Flavor network and the principles of food pairing
items.summary: Constructs a flavor network based on ingredient sharing in recipes, revealing universal principles of food pairing and culinary culture.

items.title: Deterministic scale-free networks
items.journal: Physica A Statistical Mechanics and its Applications
items.summary: Introduces deterministic models for scale-free networks that generate exact power-law degree distributions without stochastic processes.

items.title: Weighted Evolving Networks
items.summary: Extends the Barabási-Albert model to weighted evolving networks, showing how link weights co-evolve with network topology.

items.title: The sound of many hands clapping
items.summary: Reveals the physics of rhythmic applause, showing how clapping frequency synchronizes through social interactions.

items.title: Evolutionary conservation of motif constituents in the yeast protein interaction network
items.summary: Demonstrates evolutionary conservation of network motif constituents in the yeast protein interaction network.

items.title: Network-based prediction of protein interactions
items.summary: Creates a network-based computational framework for predicting protein-protein interactions, significantly expanding the coverage of the human interactome.

items.title: Target control of complex networks
items.summary: Extends network controllability theory to target control, showing that controlling a subset of nodes is more efficient than full control.

items.summary: Introduces the DIAMOnD algorithm for identifying disease modules in the human interactome from genome-wide association data.

items.summary: Shows how intracellular crowding determines the mode and sequence of substrate uptake by E. coli.

items.summary: Interprets cancer genomes by analyzing systematic perturbations of host networks by tumor virus proteins.

items.summary: Reviews control principles of complex systems, covering structural controllability, target control, and the observability of complex networks.
